sanskrit:
रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम्।
तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम्॥
transliteration: rajo rāgātmakaṃ viddhi tṛṣṇā-saṅga-samudbhavam / tannibadhnāti kaunteya karma-saṅgena dehinam
literal_meaning: Know rajas to be of the nature of desire, born from craving and attachment; it binds the embodied one through attachment to action, O Kaunteya.
contextual_meaning: The mind caught in passion is fueled by endless wanting and clings to outcomes; this craving chain-links the person to an ever-accelerating treadmill of doing and achieving.
psychological_state: Hyper-stimulated, reward-seeking, anxiety-driven productivity that masks a deeper fear of worthlessness.
trigger_scenario: Checking work email at 2 a.m. to secure a promotion while secretly fearing you'll never feel 'arrived.'
anchor_text: Desire chains you to action; act, but don’t cling to the outcome.
